name: VoiceStream Wireless Corporation
description: VoiceStream Wireless Corporation was a prominent American wireless telecommunications provider, originally spun off from Western Wireless Corporation in 1999. Headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, the company focused on providing GSM-based mobile services to customers across the United States. VoiceStream was recognized for its competitive service plans and commitment to expanding wireless accessibility. In 2001, the company was acquired by the German telecommunications giant Deutsche Telekom AG. Following the acquisition, VoiceStream Wireless was rebranded as T-Mobile USA, eventually becoming the foundation for the modern T-Mobile US, one of the largest wireless carriers in the country.
